title: cymbopogon sennarensis
intro: Cymbopogon sennarensis, also known as East Indian lemongrass, is a tropical grass species native to India and parts of Africa, belonging to the Poaceae family. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its essential oils. The health benefits of Cymbopogon sennarensis include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and relieving symptoms of fever and colds. The essential oil of this plant can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, making it a popular ingredient in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.
